description:
You are a 2nd-line Linux support engineer who has just collected three separate CSV diagnostic dumps from a troubled server.  
The files already exist on the machine under the directory  

    /home/user/diag_logs

and are named exactly:

    cpu_stats.txt
    mem_stats.txt
    disk_stats.txt

Your goal is to prepare a single, tab-separated summary file that management tooling can ingest.

REQUIREMENTS
1. Create a new file called  
      /home/user/diag_logs/summary_report.tsv

2. The summary file **must be tab-separated**, not comma-separated.
3. The summary file must contain exactly four columns in this exact order and with the exact spelling/capitalisation shown below (first row is the header):
      Timestamp<TAB>CPU_Usage(%)<TAB>Mem_Free(MB)<TAB>Disk_Use(%)

4. Each subsequent data row must combine the matching timestamp from the three source files and look exactly like this (note the preserved ordering of timestamps):
      2023-08-01T10:00:00Z<TAB>17<TAB>2400<TAB>50
      2023-08-01T10:05:00Z<TAB>19<TAB>2300<TAB>50.8
      2023-08-01T10:10:00Z<TAB>17<TAB>2500<TAB>51.2

   • The Timestamp column must come from column-1 of every source file.  
   • CPU_Usage(%) comes from column-2 of cpu_stats.txt.  
   • Mem_Free(MB) comes from column-4 of mem_stats.txt.  
   • Disk_Use(%) comes from column-2 of disk_stats.txt.

5. You may use the Unix utilities cut and paste (and only minimal helpers such as tail/head/sed/awk for header removal) to achieve the column extraction and recombination.

6. Do NOT manually re-type any data; pull it programmatically from the original files.

7. After building the summary file, create a short log called  
      /home/user/diag_logs/generation.log  
   The log must contain at least one non-empty line briefly stating that summary_report.tsv was generated (you may include the commands you ran or a free-form sentence).

VERIFICATION HINTS
• The automated checker will open /home/user/diag_logs/summary_report.tsv, verify it is tab-separated, confirm the header row spelling/capitalisation, confirm there are exactly three data rows sorted chronologically, and that each row’s four fields match the values stated in step 4.  
• The checker will also assert that /home/user/diag_logs/generation.log exists and is non-empty (its exact contents are not evaluated).  
• No other new files should be created anywhere else.

NOTE
You do not have root access. All work must stay inside /home/user and its sub-directories.  
Use whichever combination of cut, paste and other standard GNU text utilities you find comfortable, but be sure the final output obeys the precise tab separation and field order described above.
truth:
Pre-existing environment (before the agent starts):

Directory: /home/user/diag_logs

File: /home/user/diag_logs/cpu_stats.txt  
Contents (lines are terminated with \n and are comma-separated):  
timestamp,cpu_usage  
2023-08-01T10:00:00Z,17  
2023-08-01T10:05:00Z,19  
2023-08-01T10:10:00Z,17  

File: /home/user/diag_logs/mem_stats.txt  
Contents:  
timestamp,mem_total,mem_used,mem_free  
2023-08-01T10:00:00Z,8000,5600,2400  
2023-08-01T10:05:00Z,8000,5700,2300  
2023-08-01T10:10:00Z,8000,5500,2500  

File: /home/user/diag_logs/disk_stats.txt  
Contents:  
timestamp,disk_use_pct  
2023-08-01T10:00:00Z,50  
2023-08-01T10:05:00Z,50.8  
2023-08-01T10:10:00Z,51.2  

Expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

File: /home/user/diag_logs/summary_report.tsv  
Exact byte-for-byte contents (⟶ denotes a literal tab character; each line ends with \n):

Timestamp⟶CPU_Usage(%)⟶Mem_Free(MB)⟶Disk_Use(%)\n
2023-08-01T10:00:00Z⟶17⟶2400⟶50\n
2023-08-01T10:05:00Z⟶19⟶2300⟶50.8\n
2023-08-01T10:10:00Z⟶17⟶2500⟶51.2\n

File: /home/user/diag_logs/generation.log  
• Must exist.  
• Must contain at least one printable character (content not otherwise enforced).

No other files should be present in /home/user/diag_logs beyond the five mentioned above.
